title: $instanceproperties
text: $instanceproperties Return or set the current runtime widget properties of current instance. $instanceproperties { ( ) } $instanceproperties { ( ) } = PropertyValuesList Example: $instanceproperties = "Frame=T;forecolor=blue" Parameters It takes no parameter as input and its only applied on the current running instance. Data Type Description PropertyValuesList String Associative list of Property = PropertyValue pairs (separated by GOLD ; ), where PropertyValue is the value to be assigned to the property identified by Property . Return Values Associative list of widget properties for the current component instance. Only properties that have been specified in PropertyValuesList are returned. Empty string ("") if no widget properties have been specified in PropertyValuesList . If an error occurs, $procerror contains a negative value that identifies the exact error. Table 1. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following Component-Level ProcScript Functions Value Meaning -1000 Syntax error (Wrong number of arguments) Use Allowed in all component types except self-contained services. Description The function $instanceproperties sets or returns the runtime widget properties (as part of the websave JSON stream) for the current component instance. Only this instance is affected. $instanceproperties can only be used to set/get dynamic properties. For each widget property, Uniface looks for it in the property string returned by $instanceproperties . If the property is not there, it is sought in the definition time properties, and finally in the .ini properties. Restoring Default Property Values in DSPs In dynamic server pages (DSPs), it is possible to restore the default value of a property by preceding it with an exclamation mark: $instanceproperties() = " ! Property " For example: $instanceproperties() = "!style:background-color" Copy The default value is the value that the property had the first time webdefinitions was called. Thereafter, the default value is remembered by the browser and can no longer be changed. For UX Widgets, if the default value of a property is not specified, the dataUpdate() API function will receive a data including style property background-color with value uniface.RESET , which is a predefined value. When this case is executed, the application resets the value of the widget property to its initial value. For more details, see the Predefined property value: uniface.RESET section in dataUpdate . $instanceproperties in a Web Application Style references are stored as property values in the Repository, and can be manipulated with $componentproperties and $instanceproperties . For dynamic changes to the look and feel of style attributes, use the subclass component-level property, which is checked at runtime and propagated through the generated HTML. The subclass property uses predefined style references from the Cascading Style Sheet (CSS) used by the Web application. The syntax used by $instanceproperties is: $instanceproperties( )="subclass= MyClass " , where MyClass is a predefined style class in the application’s CSS. Example: Using $instanceproperties This example demonstrates how two instances of the same component can have different background colors by setting $instanceproperties based on the instance name. Explain this code // In DSP "MYDSP" operation exec newinstance "ComponentName", "compInstance1" newinstance "ComponentName", "compInstance2" activate "compInstance1".compOper() activate "compInstance2".compOper() end // In DSP "ComponentName" operation compOper if($instancename == "compInstance1") ;for compInstance1 sets backgroundColor=blue $instanceproperties() = "backgroundColor=blue" else if($instancename == "compInstance2") ;for compInstance2 sets backgroundColor=green $instanceproperties() = "backgroundColor=green" endif end Copy Related Links $componentproperties Return or set the definition-time widget properties of the current component instance.
